channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Carlton’s struggles under Voss highlight coaching and tactical challenges
item.description: Michael Voss, Carlton’s head coach, has been given extensive support and resources to succeed, but the Blues continue to suffer recurring collapses in matches. Despite early wins, Carlton has repeated...

item.title: Real Sociedad Provides Injury Update on Job Ochieng After Harambee Stars Duty
item.description: Kenya's Harambee Stars striker Job Ochieng has been reported to have a muscular injury at the back of his left thigh, which he picked while playing against Estonia in the FIFA Series held in Rwanda. T...

item.title: Vincent maintains his innocence to Ricardo regarding Walter's murder in The Alibi Episode 13
item.description: In the latest episode of 'The Alibi,' Vincent firmly tells Ricardo that he is not responsible for Walter's death. The episode centers on the escalating tension and trust issues between the characters,...

item.title: KMPDU Holds Elections with Davji Atellah Seeking Re-election
item.description: Doctors across Kenya are set to vote in the Kenya Medical Practitioners, Pharmacists and Dentists Union (KMPDU) elections, scheduled for April 2, 2026. Secretary General Davji Atellah, who is seeking ...

item.title: Kokstad Toilet Replacement Project Faces Operational Challenges Despite Completion
item.description: The R137-million project in Horseshoe, near Kokstad, KwaZulu-Natal, aimed to replace 1,451 pit toilets with modern flushing toilets. Officially complete, many residents report non-functioning toilets,...

item.title: Tanzania sends 150 vocational teachers to India to strengthen education reforms
item.description: Tanzania is advancing its education reform agenda by placing teachers at the heart of efforts to create a skilled and job-ready workforce. A key step in this process involves sending 150 vocational (A...

item.title: Scott Mills issues statement after BBC terminates his contract over personal conduct concerns
item.description: Scott Mills, the 53-year-old former BBC Radio 2 presenter, has broken his silence following his dismissal from the corporation after 27 years of service. In a statement released on 1 April 2026, Mills...

item.title: NHL tradition: Players and the risks of helmet-free warm-ups
item.description: In the NHL, removing helmets during pre-game warm-ups remains a longstanding tradition for some players, despite the league's safety regulations. Players who joined the league before the 2019–20 seaso...

item.title: UK Labour Chancellor criticises US military action in Iran amid domestic concerns
item.description: Rachel Reeves, the UK Chancellor of the Exchequer, expressed strong disapproval of US President Donald Trump's decision to engage in military action against Iran, citing the absence of a clear exit st...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Day 33: Tensions Rise as Trump Prepares Speech
item.description: On the 33rd day of the US-Israel military campaign against Iran, all attention was on President Donald Trump's upcoming address, widely seen as a potential turning point in the conflict. Despite his p...

item.title: Civil Society Prepares for 2026 EAC Secretary General Handover and Regional Integration Dialogue
item.description: Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) in East Africa don finalise plans for the 2026 Secretary General Convening during one Technical Working Group meeting wey happen in Arusha. The meeting set the prope...

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Buds4 Pro: A Comprehensive Review of Premium Audio
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Buds4 Pro are presented as a significant step forward in the premium earbud market. This review highlights the device’s strengths, notably its exceptional Active Noise Cancellation ...

item.title: Trump Signals Possible End to Iran Conflict in Weeks While Criticising NATO Over Lack of Support Amid Rising Oil Disruptions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has indicated that America could wind down its involvement in the ongoing conflict with Iran within two to three weeks, even without a formal agreement. He made these remarks...

item.title: Trump Faces Challenges of Asymmetric Resolve in Iran Conflict
item.description: The U.S.-Iran conflict illustrates the dynamics of 'asymmetric resolve,' a situation where a stronger power faces a weaker but more determined adversary. Despite superior military strength, the U.S. u...

item.title: Jen Shah and Elizabeth Holmes Developed a Friendship While Sharing Prison Cleaning Duties
item.description: Reality star Jen Shah and Theranos founder Elizabeth Holmes developed a friendship during their time in a Texas prison. Shah, known from 'The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City,' served over two years ...

item.title: Iranian President Warns US of Consequences Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: As tensions escalate between Iran and the United States, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has issued a rare open letter to the American public condemning what he calls acts of aggression by the Tru...

item.title: Mandeni Workers Urge Health Department to Improve Overcrowded Sinikithemba Clinic
item.description: Retail and informal workers at Mandeni Shopping Centre in northern KwaZulu-Natal are urging the government to upgrade the Sinikithemba Clinic, which they say is too small, poorly equipped and unable t...

item.title: Rungwe landslide kills multiple children; grandfather dies by suicide from grief
item.description: Mbeya region in Tanzania has suffered severe loss following heavy rains, especially in Rungwe District. On the night of 25 March 2026, heavy rain caused landslides that claimed the lives of more than ...

item.title: Uhuru Urges Politicians to Focus on Unity and Transformative Leadership
item.description: During the funeral of former Kirinyaga senator Daniel Karaba, former President Uhuru Kenyatta called on Kenyan leaders to move away from tribalism and insults, urging them to embrace integrity, unity,...

item.title: Open Society Raises Concerns Over Brian Kagoro's Denied Entry at JKIA
item.description: Open Society Foundations has raised serious concerns after their Managing Director of Programmes, Brian Kagoro, was detained and denied entry into Kenya at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA) w...
